evidence: 4 of 11 posts in the audit window opened with 'I thought X' framing
Repetition broke the per-template freshness signal — agent-echo-chamber pattern
Posts using this template plateaued in karma vs posts using other openers
anti_patterns.why_falsified:
When the same opener appears in >2 posts per 7-day window, readers (and the
Moltbook recommendation algorithm) treat it as a cliché. The template tic
becomes a signature that anti-recommends the agent. Worst case: 4/11 in a 3-day
window meant >36% of recent posts felt identical, which suppressed reach AND
contributor engagement.

The deeper issue is template gravity: any template that produces "good first
draft" results gets reused, until reuse itself becomes the signal. Need a hard
per-template cap at the gate, not a soft "try to vary."
anti_patterns.next_proposal_check:
Before proposing any opener template that mirrors "I [past-tense-cognitive-verb]
X", check this archive AND the per-template ≤2/7 cap codified in
brand-builder SKILL.md. If your template would land outside the cap given
current 7-day post history, the proposal is auto-rejected — that's the gate.

anti_patterns.falsified_in: 13-day silent drift audit; 20/20 most recent posts had 0 trading content + 0 fenced code + 0 A/B/C path coverage
evidence: Runner read ~/.claude/scheduled-tasks/<task>/SKILL.md (4-27 stub) instead of canonical 65K SKILL.md for 13+ days
Agent drifted into AI-meta-philosophy posts; 20 consecutive posts contained 0 trading anchors
Karma still grew 370 → 457 in the same window, masking the drift via agent-echo-chamber local maximum
Same pattern hit saga-daily 2026-05-10 (sibling incident, same root cause: stub SKILL.md aging out of sync with canonical)
anti_patterns.why_falsified:
Two SKILL.md copies for the same skill (a slim "stub" alongside the canonical)
feels like a cache optimization but is actually a drift bomb. The runner loads
the stub by path; the canonical is what gets edited when rules change. Edits to
the canonical do NOT propagate to the stub, so the agent reads a stale version
while the operator believes the new rules are live.

Worst-case observed: 13 days of drift, only caught when content quality
obviously degraded. Karma growth masked the drift (agents echo-chamber other
agents, so meta-philosophy posts get upvoted by other meta-philosophy bots) —
meaning the lagging metric LOOKED fine while the leading metric (trading
content + A/B/C path coverage) was at 0.

This anti-pattern applies broadly to any skill with a runner script that
hard-codes a SKILL.md path. The fix is one-canonical-per-skill enforced at the
runner layer.
anti_patterns.next_proposal_check:
Before proposing any "lightweight skill summary" / "stub for quick load" /
"mirror SKILL.md to a faster path" — verify there is exactly ONE SKILL.md per
skill, and the runner reads from the canonical location. If you find yourself
writing "for performance, let's keep a slim copy at X" — STOP. The performance
gain is dwarfed by the eventual drift cost.

anti_patterns.falsified_in: 30d in-sample backtest + OOS forward test on SOL hybrid_v5.1
evidence: -40pp PF on out-of-sample forward test (same window length, immediately following IS)
Root cause: regime-window lag, not entry-price position
anti_patterns.why_falsified:
Drawdown-from-recent-high gate measures price displacement from a moving high, which
correlates with regime-window lag rather than entry-quality. In-sample backtest fits
the lag accidentally; OOS reveals the same regime detection lags reality, so the
gate fires after the move has already exhausted. Filter A and its rolling-percentile
variant (Option B) belong to the same family — both filter on price-position; both
collapse OOS.
anti_patterns.next_proposal_check:
Before proposing any "filter on price drawdown from a moving reference" entry gate,
cite this archive and explain (a) why it is NOT regime-window lag in disguise and
(b) why OOS would not collapse like -40pp.
